quranicRelevance: The miraculous she-camel of Saleh (Naqah - 'hathihi naqatu Allahi'), and warnings against harming her ('naqata Allahi wa suqyaha').
figurativeOrRareUsages: نَاقَةُ اللَّهِ وَسُقْيَاهَا (91:13): The she-camel of God and her drink-access (symbol of divine property and test of Thamud).
discardedNoise: Coordination and neatness (Tanseeq) in modern layout designs.
summaryEn: The root refers to she-camels (Naqah), focusing on the sacred she-camel of Saleh which served as a national test and sign for Thamud.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى النوق (ناقة)، مركزاً حصراً على ناقة صالح الإعجازية التي مثلت آية إلهية واختباراً مصيرياً لقوم ثمود.
rawText: تَنَّيَقَ فِى مَطْعَمِهِ وَمَلْبَسِهِ 5 , and نَنَوَّقَ, He was nice and luxurious (تَجَوَّدَ وَبَالَغَ) in his diet and his apparel. (JK, K.) Better explained voce تَأَنَّقَ. === تَنَوَّقَ: see تَأَنَّقَ and تَجَوَّدَ, and نِيقَةٌ. نَاقَةٌ : see قَلُوصٌ, and بَعِيرٌ, and بَكْرٌ. === نَاقَةُ الحَذَّآءِ: see رِحَالَةٌ. === أَيْنُقَاتٌ pl. of أَيْنُقٌ pl. of نَاقَةٌ: see a verse cited voce مُسْهَمٌ. نَوَّقَ 2 [He broke, or trained, a camel]. (TA, voce مُصْعَبٌ.) نِيقٌ quasi-inf. n. of أَنَاقَ. نِيقَةٌ a subst. from تَنَوَّقَ (IJ, S, K) as syn. with تَأَنَّقَ (S) [and therefore signifying Daintiness, nicety, exquisiteness, refinement, or scrupulous nicety and exactness; and the exceeding of what is usual in a thing: or the choosing what is excellent, or best, to be done, and doing admirably: or the doing firmly, solidly, soundly, or thoroughly, and skilfully: or] the exceeding what is usual in a thing, and making it good, or beautiful, and firm, solid, sound, or free from defect or imperfection. (Ham, p. 625) See تَأَنَّقَ.
